description_long: This house features a classic European townhouse design with a steep, dark gabled roof and two prominent brick chimneys. The front facade is symmetrical, with four large windows on the upper floor and two on the lower, flanking a central double-door entryway. The exterior walls are finished in a muted beige or light gray stucco, and the roof includes two dormer windows that add architectural interest. The side and rear elevations are simpler, with minimal window openings and plain wall surfaces, suggesting a focus on the street-facing facade. The structure appears to be two stories tall, with a rectangular main volume and an attached wing extending from one side. The overall appearance is solid and traditional, suitable for an urban residential neighborhood.
description: A two-story European-style townhouse with a steep gabled roof, symmetrical windows, and a central double-door entry, finished in muted stucco and brick.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical facade, four upper and two lower windows, central double-door entry, and two dormer windows on the steep roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain stucco wall with no windows and the extension of the building's wing.
description_view_2: Angled front view highlighting the main entry, window arrangement, and the attached side wing.
description_view_3: Rear and side view showing the plain back wall, the extension, and the two brick chimneys.
attribute_entryway_design: Central double-door entry with no porch, flanked by windows and topped by a simple lintel.
